name: X-Force: Internal Host Communicating with Host Categorized as Anonymizer
notes:
This rule triggers when a local host connects to a host that has been identified as an anonymous proxy. This is typically a policy issue.

Note: The Confidence Factor ranges between 0 to 100. On assets of lower importance, an administrator might weigh an X-Force rule to trigger on a higher confidence factor of 75. When tuning rules, customers should think of a scale where 50 is the tipping point.

See the blog https://ibm.biz/BdPEXv for details.
conditions_text: when the context is Local to Remote
when Destination IP is categorized by X-Force as Anonymization Services with confidence value greater than 75
TD_list: APPLY X-Force: Internal Host Communicating with Host Categorized as Anonymizer on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the context is Local to Remote
AND when Destination IP is categorized by X-Force as Anonymization Services with confidence value greater than 75
RED_list: Event name: Internal HostsCommunicating with Host Categorized as Anonymizer
Event description: This rule will identify when internal hosts connect to a host that has been identified as an anonymous proxy. This is typically a policy issue.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
